message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="luca-palmieri" color="blue" summary="Focus management and visible label fixes for re-review">
Heydon, I've addressed both of your blocking items and pushed (CI green, commit e2c292e).

Changes for your review:

**1. Focus management after complete/delete (your blocking #1):**
- Created `static/js/next-action-focus.js` -- external script (replaced inline scripts)
- On `htmx:beforeSwap`, captures the focus target before DOM removal:
  - Next sibling `.next-action-item` (if one exists)
  - Previous sibling `.next-action-item` (if deleted was last in list)
  - The `#next-action-title` input (if the list is now empty)
- On `htmx:afterSettle`, applies focus to the captured target
- Also handles inline edit focus: entering edit mode focuses the edit input, saving/canceling focuses the edit link
- Makes non-focusable elements focusable with `tabindex="-1"` when needed

**2. Visible label on context select (your blocking #2):**
- Replaced `aria-label="Context"` on the `<select>` with a visible `<label for="next-action-context" class="next-actions__context-label">Context</label>`
- Added integration test verifying the visible label exists

Please re-review when you can.
</teammate-message>

content.text:
Both blocking items are now properly resolved. Let me verify each:

**Blocking 1 -- Focus management: RESOLVED**

`static/js/next-action-focus.js` correctly implements the full focus management pattern:
- Line 35-72: `htmx:beforeSwap` captures focus targets before DOM removal
- Line 38: Correctly scopes to `.next-action-item` elements only
- Lines 45-58: Empty response (complete/delete) captures next sibling, then previous sibling, then falls back to `#next-action-title`
- Lines 62-65: Edit mode entry targets the edit input via selector
- Lines 68-71: Edit mode exit targets the edit link on the restored item
- Lines 74-104: `htmx:afterSettle` applies focus, adding `tabindex="-1"` when needed for non-focusable elements
- Line 103: The template loads it as an external script (`<script src="/static/js/next-action-focus.js"></script>`), replacing the inline scripts

**Blocking 2 -- Visible label for context select: RESOLVED**

Line 61-62: `<label for="next-action-context" class="next-actions__context-label">Context</label>` is now a visible label properly associated with the `<select>`. The `aria-label` attribute has been correctly removed from the `<select>`.

input.content:
Heydon Pickering -- Re-review: BOTH BLOCKING ITEMS RESOLVED

**Blocking 1 (Focus management): Resolved.**
`static/js/next-action-focus.js` correctly implements the HTMX Focus Management Protocol:
- Captures next/previous siblings in `htmx:beforeSwap` before DOM removal
- Applies focus in `htmx:afterSettle` with proper `tabindex="-1"` fallback
- Falls back to `#next-action-title` when the list becomes empty
- Handles edit mode entry (focus edit input) and exit (focus edit link)
- Loaded as external script -- clean separation from template

**Blocking 2 (Visible context label): Resolved.**
The `<select>` now has a visible `<label for="next-action-context">Context</label>` and the `aria-label` has been correctly removed.

I'm satisfied with these fixes. CONSENSUS APPROVED from Heydon Pickering (Accessibility Specialist).

Note: My original minor items (#3 Save/Cancel unique names, #4 Unicode checkbox) and nice-to-haves (#5 context badge association, #6 empty state announcement) remain as-is. I'm fine deferring those -- none are user barriers. The implementation meets WCAG 2.2 AA for the core interactions.
input.summary: Both blocking items resolved -- CONSENSUS APPROVED

content.text:
Both blocking items are verified as resolved. I've sent my **CONSENSUS APPROVED** to Luca and notified the team lead.

**Summary of what was fixed:**
1. `static/js/next-action-focus.js` -- Full focus management for HTMX swaps (complete, delete, edit enter/exit), following the same proven pattern from `todo-focus.js`
2. `templates/next_actions.html:61-62` -- Visible `<label for="next-action-context">Context</label>` replacing the `aria-label` on the select

The implementation now meets WCAG 2.2 AA for all core interactions. Minor items (unique Save/Cancel names, Unicode checkbox robustness) and nice-to-haves (context badge association, empty state announcement) are deferred as non-blocking.
